records.rationale: The advisory marks bigint-buffer versions through 1.1.5 as vulnerable and lists >=1.1.6 as patched, but npm currently publishes no 1.1.6 or later release. The production Wormhole/Solana paths remain on bigint-buffer@1.1.5; forcing the nonexistent patched version would break reproducible installation.
records.remediation: Adopt the first upstream bigint-buffer release containing the fix, or replace the Wormhole/Solana dependency path with a validated alternative before the exception expires.

approval.reference: CON-1525; GitHub #399 (tracking context only; not approval evidence)

records.rationale: The advisory marks elliptic versions through 6.6.1 as vulnerable and lists >=6.6.2 as patched, but npm currently publishes no 6.6.2 release. The production Wormhole/CosmJS and Payjoin paths remain on elliptic@6.6.1 while the dependency paths are evaluated for a compatible fix.
records.remediation: Adopt the first upstream elliptic release containing the fix, or replace the affected Wormhole/CosmJS and Payjoin dependency paths with validated alternatives before the exception expires.

approval.reference: CON-1525; GitHub #399 (tracking context only; not approval evidence)

records.rationale: This advisory is confined to the local Vite development-server response-read surface. Production and release artifacts do not run or expose that development server.
evidence: The production command is `pnpm run build`, which emits the static `dist` artifact and does not start a Vite development server.
The `Web build` step in `.github/workflows/android-release.yml` runs `pnpm run build` before Android packaging; the release workflow has no Vite development-server step.
esbuild@0.28.1 was tested and breaks `vite-plugin-top-level-await` production builds, so esbuild@0.27.3 is retained for compatibility while this advisory remains explicitly tracked.
